item.title: Anthropic agrees terms of $30bn funding deal at $900bn valuation
item.description: AI lab has tapped Dragoneer, Greenoaks, Sequoia Capital and Altimeter Capital to lead new round

item.title: Asia-Pacific Innovative Lawyers Awards 2026: the winners
item.description: Awards highlights include: restructuring, unlocking capital, digital strategy and disputes. Plus the most innovative law firms and individuals

item.title: Innovative Lawyers: Asia-Pacific
item.description: The FT’s Top 30 law firms in the region. Plus: legal skills are in demand to deal with challenges including US-China tensions and opportunities such as Hong Kong’s IPO boom; and best-practice case studies

item.title: Practice of law: case studies
item.description: Examples of the most innovative work and legal services for clients in the Asia-Pacific region

item.title: Business of law: case studies
item.description: Examples of the most innovative work and legal services for clients in the Asia-Pacific region

item.title: A record of imagination and skill in unusual deals
item.description: The practitioners featured here have all achieved breakthroughs in their legal work, but one in particular stood out

item.title: Australian law firms are taking a lead on navigating best use of AI
item.description: Leaders are focusing on how their business models will change. Plus: the top 30 innovative law firms ranked

item.title: US law firms rethink their strategy for Greater China
item.description: Fees are a ‘pain point’ and a main driver of exits by foreign outfits

item.title: Fallout from China housing slowdown grinds on in offshore courts
item.description: Bondholders might balk at the recoveries offered so far, but any remedies involve complex negotiations

item.title: Japanese text opens new legal tech chapter
item.description: The intrapreneurs highlighted here all have an entrepreneurial approach to law firm operation, but one in particular stood out

item.title: The growth of ‘build-your-own’ legal AI tools
item.description: Law firms are developing their own systems, sometimes with an eye to selling them to clients

item.title: Hong Kong listings boom sparks legal hiring spree
item.description: The revival in deal activity has law firms rebuilding their teams

item.title: In-house legal teams step up on AI strategies
item.description: Good foundations are essential in embracing new tech opportunities. Plus, case studies highlighting the most innovative work achieved by in-house teams in Asia-Pacific

item.title: Bollywood stars fight identity theft
item.description: Aishwarya Rai Bachchan is among Indian celebrities whose cases are shaping laws to curb AI-fuelled fake online content

item.title: Japan Inc sets its sights on global peers
item.description: Law firms stand to benefit, but they face their own hiring and expansion challenges

item.title: US-China tensions add to growing complexity of disputes work
item.description: Lawyers are adapting by developing tech expertise and fighting cases in several jurisdictions

item.title: LVMH to sell Marc Jacobs to WHP
item.description: Sale is latest in a series of disposals by Paris-based group of smaller, underperforming brands

item.title: Banks line up to back hospitality billionaire’s deal for Caesars Entertainment
item.description: Owner of NBA’s Houston Rockets and Keens Steakhouse edges closer in pursuit of one of Las Vegas Strip’s icons

item.title: Xpeng in talks with VW about buying a factory in Europe
item.description: Carmaker dubbed ‘China’s Tesla’ also explores building a new plant on the continent

item.title: OpenAI considering legal action against Apple over iPhone AI deal
item.description: ChatGPT maker believes tech giant has failed to invest sufficiently in their partnership

item.title: US authorities prepare to end Adani fraud cases
item.description: Indian tycoon had lobbied Trump administration and pledged billions of dollars of investment in America

item.title: AI chipmaker Cerebras surges more than 100% in Wall Street debut
item.description: Trading pop underscores strong demand for shares in companies linked to AI boom

item.title: Ford shares surge after launch of power unit for data centres
item.description: New subsidiary pivots to energy storage batteries for AI after disastrous electric vehicle writedown

item.title: OpenAI trial hinges on Sam Altman’s credibility, says Elon Musk lawyer
item.description: Lawyers for world’s richest man try to paint AI founder as untrustworthy in closing arguments in court case

item.title: Burberry tried to be Louis Vuitton; it should aim lower
item.description: The historic brand’s rarefied look didn’t sell; and it is returning to its classic trench coats, scarves and check pattern

item.title: Israeli leaders threaten New York Times with lawsuit
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu says he will take legal action over article detailing sexual abuse of Palestinians by Israeli prison guards

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover swings to an annual loss after cyber attack and US tariffs
item.description: Tata Motors-owned luxury car maker’s chief executive vows to make it ‘more resilient’

item.title: UK company linked to Kazakh oligarch found liable in employment tribunal
item.description: Financial services group Jusan withheld termination fee from former chief operating officer, judge rules

item.title: Reader callout: Do you regret buying your home?
item.description: Some buyers find themselves living in properties bought during the pandemic rush but have come to regret their decision.

item.title: Hargreaves Lansdown to cut jobs in bid to fight off digital rivals
item.description: UK’s biggest DIY investment platform plans to spend £100mn to improve products and digital services
